title: malus pumila var. praecox
intro: Malus pumila var. praecox, also known as the dwarf crab apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Rosaceae. The bark and leaves of the dwarf crab apple have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Malus pumila var. praecox include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
